title: thymelaea tarton-raira subsp. austroiberica
intro: Thymelaea tarton-raira subsp. austroiberica, also known as the yellow spurge, is a plant species within the Thymelaeaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Iberian Peninsula, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Thymelaea tarton-raira subsp. austroiberica can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with its leaves and roots being the most commonly used parts for medicinal purposes.
